title: Build Test Validation Flow
description: End-to-end build correctness validation: run build script against fixture data, verify generated output files and content correctness

test_cases: should call readSourceFiles with root directory
should call all transformers with correct arguments
integration: full build creates all expected outputs
should call transformers in correct order
should include agents and kiro transformers

activity_flow: SETUP: Create temp dir TEST_DIR = 'test-tmp-build' (beforeEach: rmSync + mkdirSync)
FIXTURE CREATION [integration tests only]: Write SKILL.md to TEST_DIR/source/skills/<name>/SKILL.md
FIXTURE FORMAT: frontmatter block (---) with fields: name, description, license, user-invocable, argument-hint; then body text with {{placeholder}} tokens
readSourceFiles scans source/skills/ entries (each a directory)
For each skill dir: read SKILL.md, parseFrontmatter() → { frontmatter, body }
Also reads reference/ subdir (.md files) and scripts/ subdir into arrays
Returns skill objects: { name, description, license, userInvocable, argumentHint, body, references, scripts, ... }
Parses ### Section headings and DO/DON'T lines per section
TRANSFORM [ordered sequence, cursor first]: transformers.transformCursor(skills, DIST_DIR, patterns)
TRANSFORM [optional in test]: transformers.transformAgents(skills, DIST_DIR, patterns)
TRANSFORM [optional in test]: transformers.transformKiro(skills, DIST_DIR, patterns)
Cursor content: contains frontmatter (---), name: audit, {{target}} preserved, {{model}} → 'the model'
Claude Code content: user-invocable: true present, {{target}} preserved, {{model}} → 'Claude'
VALIDATE EMPTY SKILLS: all transformers called with ([], DIST_DIR, patterns) when no skills found
VALIDATE MULTIPLE SKILLS: skills.length === 2; all four providers emit files for both skill1, skill2

key_functions.utils.readSourceFiles(rootDir): Scans rootDir/source/skills/, parses SKILL.md frontmatter+body, reads reference/ and scripts/ subdirs. Returns { skills[] }
key_functions.utils.readPatterns(rootDir): Reads rootDir/source/skills/impeccable/SKILL.md, parses DO/DON'T lines by ### section. Returns { patterns[], antipatterns[] }
key_functions.utils.parseFrontmatter(content): Regex-based YAML parser for --- blocks. Returns { frontmatter{}, body }

placeholder_replacements.{{target}}: preserved as-is (not a provider placeholder — argument from user invocation)

fixture_data_format.body_example: Please audit {{target}} for technical quality. Ask {{model}} for help.

branching_conditions: IF skills.length === 0 → transformers still called with empty array (no-op output)
IF skills.length > 0 → each transformer writes one SKILL.md per skill per provider
IF reference/ dir exists → references array populated in skill object
IF scripts/ dir exists → scripts array populated in skill object
IF source/skills/impeccable/SKILL.md missing → readPatterns returns empty { patterns:[], antipatterns:[] }
spy_mocking_pattern: bun:test spyOn used to mock utils.readSourceFiles, utils.readPatterns, and all transformers in unit tests; integration tests run real implementations against real filesystem fixtures
transformer_creation: All transformers are factory-created: createTransformer(providerConfig) from scripts/lib/transformers/factory.js; provider configs defined in scripts/lib/transformers/providers.js
key_relationships: transformers/index.js exports createTransformer(PROVIDERS.X) for each provider
utils.readPatterns reads impeccable/SKILL.md via utils pattern
important_notes: Transformer invocation ORDER is enforced: cursor → claude-code → gemini → codex. Content transformation preserves user-argument placeholders ({{target}}) but replaces system placeholders ({{model}}, {{config_file}}, {{ask_instruction}}). 'user-invocable: true' frontmatter field controls Claude Code argument-hint inclusion.
